explicit_text: I was born in Virginia, Illinois on April 1, 1940. It's a very small rural town in south central Illinois with less than a thousand people, surrounded by farmland. Although I only lived there for about two years and don't remember much, I have visited since then to see the place where I was born.

explicit_text: In 1941, when I was about a year old, my sister Marian and I were taken to live at The Baby Fold, an orphanage in Normal, Illinois. I stayed there with my sister for about six or seven months in 1942 before being adopted. The Baby Fold is still operating in Normal, and I even gave a talk at their Founder's Day banquet recently, which was an emotional experience given how much adoption has shaped my life.

explicit_text: After living in Clinton until 1948, my family moved to Oneida, Illinois, which is about fifty miles north of Peoria. I lived there through my childhood and graduated from high school in 1958. Oneida was my home during my school years after the move from Clinton.
